result:
Done — the dotted surface is now scoped to the bottom of the page and verified in the browser with no console errors.

What changed:
- The dot-wave `<div>` now lives inside the final "Reserve" section as an absolutely-positioned background layer (`overflow:clip` on the section keeps it contained), instead of a fixed layer covering the whole viewport. The section's text and form sit above it on their own layer.
- The three.js renderer and camera now size to that section rather than `window`, including on resize.
- Restored the hero's original opaque background from level-3, so the top of the page looks exactly as it did before — the wave only appears in the bottom CTA area (still animated, always on, no trigger).

Verified both ends: the hero is back to its clean level-3 look, and the wave renders behind the reserve form at the bottom. Level-3 remains untouched.
